title: Argo implementation protocol — how an authorized plan becomes accepted code

authority.vocabulary: docs/mythological-vocabulary.md#Argo (carries an authorized plan through implementation, verification, and documentation)

steps: load the records in AI-START-HERE.md order (authority -> manifest -> active plan -> invariants/non-claims -> decisions)
entry_ritual.rule: no implementation authority until the comprehension gate exits 0 — and a passing artifact is the PRECONDITION for authority, not the authority itself; The Eye grants

slice_path: bounded slice scoped by the frozen, authorized plan
deterministic slice gate must reach 'meets' (disk-verified facts, fail-closed)
signed required-seat review (claude/agy/codex required, grok/gemini advisory; trust_mode signed) — as many rounds as The Eye directs
The Eye's acceptance (may bound a lone dissent with a stopping rule + a recorded deferred minor-fix backlog)
merge, with merge_anchor + reviewed_head recorded in the accepted-slice ledger

dissent_asymmetry.at_authorization: a required seat's 'revise' BLOCKS (fail-closed model gate; authz-007)
dissent_asymmetry.at_slice_acceptance: The Eye MAY accept over a lone dissent with a stopping rule + deferred backlog (slice 4a)
dissent_asymmetry.why_both_are_consistent: the model gate is fail-closed because models never outrank The Eye; slice acceptance is The Eye exercising the human authority the gate exists to protect. Neither direction lets a model outrank the human.

no_autonomous_runner: Argo names a role and its protocol. There is no Argo daemon or service; every implementation slice is human-initiated work carried by whichever implementer holds a passing reader-validation artifact and The Eye's go-ahead.
why: The protocol makes succession safe: a memoryless future implementer cannot start from a confident guess (comprehension gate), cannot self-certify (slice gate + signed review re-derive from disk), and cannot land without The Eye. The asymmetry entry exists because it is the single most likely inference error about this workflow.
change_rule: Changing the entry ritual, slice path, or dissent rules is a governance change: Eye ruling -> Daedalus delta -> TELOS re-authorization (docs/institutional-memory/CHANGE-PROTOCOL.md); this contract must be updated in the same change or verify-contracts fails.

oracle.test: docs/institutional-memory/verify-contracts.mjs (EXECUTES the comprehension gate: the correct example reader must exit 0 and the hallucinating reader must exit 3; asserts the recorded slice review ran signed with the required trio and the slice gate converged at meets)
